issues.title: SDF session resource exhaustion due to per-instance allocation
issues.problem: SDF sessions are opened in the constructor and only closed via finalize(). Cryptographic hardware devices have strict limits on concurrent sessions (e.g., 256). JCE code creates many short-lived Cipher instances, and garbage collection is non-deterministic. Under load, this will exhaust device sessions, causing application-wide cryptographic failures.

issues.title: Insecure predictable temporary file extraction path
issues.problem: The native library is extracted to a predictable directory ${java.io.tmpdir}/sdf4j-jce-native/. On multi-user systems, a malicious user can pre-create this directory or replace the .so file, leading to TOCTOU race conditions and potential arbitrary code execution within the JVM process.

issues.title: sm4DecryptInit returns context after native init fails
issues.problem: When SDF_DecryptInit fails at line 507-510, the function throws an exception but continues to mark ctx->initialized = 1 and returns the context. The caller then proceeds with a half-initialized context whose key handle was already destroyed. This turns a clean init failure into follow-on errors.

issues.title: Provider constructor eagerly initializes hardware and ignores path argument
issues.problem: SDFProvider() immediately calls into native initialization, so simple provider registration fails on machines without a configured SDF device/library. The SDFProvider(String libraryPath) constructor claims backward compatibility but discards libraryPath, breaking existing callers that passed a path directly.

issues.title: Missing bounds check on offset/len parameters
issues.problem: sm4EncryptUpdate and sm4DecryptUpdate perform raw pointer arithmetic with dataBytes + offset but never verify that offset and len are within array bounds. A malicious caller can drive these methods into out-of-bounds reads and crash the JVM.

issues.title: Missing NULL check after GetByteArrayElements in sm4DecryptInit
issues.problem: GetByteArrayElements can return NULL on OutOfMemoryError. The code at line 483 fails to verify keyBytes before using it, which will cause a segmentation fault when dereferenced.

issues.title: DER parser accepts signatures with trailing garbage
issues.problem: derToRaw only checks that the available bytes are >= length, then parses two INTEGERs and returns without verifying no bytes remain. A blob like valid_signature || attacker_controlled_suffix is accepted, which is not DER-canonical.

issues.title: copyToFixedBuffer has incorrect truncation logic
issues.problem: When srcLen > fixedLen (32 bytes), the code truncates from the end. For properly encoded DER integers with a 0x00 prefix byte (making 33 bytes), this would truncate the wrong byte - the 0x00 prefix should be removed, not trailing bytes.

issues.title: SM2PublicKey exposes internal key arrays
issues.problem: getX() and getY() methods return the internal byte arrays directly instead of defensive copies, allowing external code to modify the key material.

issues.title: SM4Cipher uses deprecated finalize() for cleanup
issues.problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Combined with the session resource leak issue, resources may not be released properly. The class should implement AutoCloseable for deterministic cleanup.

issues.title: SM3MessageDigest uses deprecated finalize() for session cleanup
issues.problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Session resources may leak since each SM3MessageDigest instance opens its own SDF session in the constructor.

issues.title: SM2Signature uses deprecated finalize() for session cleanup
issues.problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Session resources may leak since each SM2Signature instance opens its own SDF session in the constructor.

issues.title: SM2KeyPairGenerator uses deprecated finalize() for session cleanup
issues.problem: The finalize() method is deprecated and unreliable. Session resources may leak since each SM2KeyPairGenerator instance opens its own SDF session in the constructor.

issues.title: hexToBytes doesn't validate input length
issues.problem: The hexToBytes method assumes the hex string has even length. If an odd-length string is passed, it will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException instead of a more descriptive error.
